item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Maybe we shouldn't take that guess, 2021 is still as insane as 2020 with the current number of crises being:

-2-

Crises 1: Covid-19
Crises 2: Nuclear treaty expiring

-Please reply to ask for more crises-

item.title: Re: 2020 Update
item.content:encoded: That would be an authorial decision. If you feel that the edit is necessary, contact the original author of the page.

item.title: 2020 Update
item.content:encoded: Could I recommend we change 1032-013 to present tense? E.g. “Researchers are attempting to discern whether or not this constitutes SCP-1032's first failure.”

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Or maybe in 2020 there will be a catastrophic event, that is not named to avoid alerting us to its identity, that will define 2020 to most of the world's population. This catastrophe will continue through 1/1/2022

That's my retcon and I'm sticking to it….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Happy 2021

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Depending on what the clock actually means by 2020 ending, it could also only come clear what this means at 01/01/2022.

Also even though this is tagged as collaboration I'm not sure whether or not it is allowed to "update" another user's attribution like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well well well, 2020 is about to end

Someone pls edit it after 2020 ends

item.title: Re: Will this infernal year ever end?
item.content:encoded: I've really gotta say, with all the talk online about accidental prophecies about 2020, reading this piece on December 31st 2020 was NOT very reassuring.

item.title: New Image!
item.content:encoded:
I love the new image, it even has small words on it.

Good job Escape From Site 19.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that the image has been replaced, as sourced on this page's licensebox, with a cc-compliant alternative.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Have no idea how I forgot to upvote this, it is fantastic!

item.title: Will this infernal year ever end?
item.content:encoded:
I thought the 2022 thing was a joke about this terrible year lasting longer, but I read it's there since a couple years. Creepily on point!
Plus: a couple of previsions expired, are they being forgotten (I know it's a quite old SCP) or does it mean something?
Anyway, really liked it
+1

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that the image could not be properly sourced. As such, it has been assumed to be non-compliant and thus removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1032-013
Current text: "2020"
Predicted date: 01/01/2022

you know what? it might as well.

item.title: SCP-1032-02
item.content:encoded: Small thing, but it says "no useful information on SCP-1032-2 is available." I believe it should be changed to "no useful information on SCP-1032-02 is available."

item.title: Re: Mod Post
item.content:encoded: Damn, I forgot I posted this. Sleep deprevation makes me do the weirdest things. My bad, obviously. (I meant to delete it; must've forgotten…)

item.title: Mod Post
item.content:encoded: And don't give arbitrarily time-limits to assume you're allowed to do shit. In fact, just don't make edits to other people's work that aren't spelling or grammar corrections, period.

item.title: Operational Staff Post
item.content:encoded: Don't.

item.title: Update Needed
item.content:encoded:
Some of the dates on these are a bit off.

Ignore the rest of this.

If there is no response from a higher-up to this by next week (September 1), I will make the edits myself.

item.title: This needs an update
item.content:encoded: many of the dates listed are long past gone. Anyone willing to update those?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree it is very interesting. Maybe he added those because he wanted people reading to think it is self-aware.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I bet theres gonna be a show called "2020" and the last episode will air on 01/01/2022

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added a -14.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1032-13 would be correct in two cases:

  1. At the close of the year 2020, a catastrophic temporal anomaly will causes the universal timeline to skip a year.
  2. At the close of the year 2021, a catastrophic temporal anomaly will erase the year 2020 from the universal timeline.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: we need the creator of this SCP to come back on Jan 1 2021 and explain what happened with the clock

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Your idea sounds like it'd make a great -J lister. The title, of course, would have to be "Killing Time".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good, but I didn't felt much about the logs of the current status of the hands. It was kind of a let down.
Well, I guess 2021 doesn't exist anymore, wonder what it'll say next.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Hi, I'm very late. I made this years ago and people have made it better, so as such, I've added the collaboration tag to it, in the hopes that people can continue doing great things, like adding more hands to the clock, and so on and so forth.

If anyone who had ideas for this is still around, then go ahead and give it a shot. I'll also try to check back a bit later and fix up some dates and such that are out of date; either adding more to the article, or maybe just pushing them to a much later time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: apparently i'm the odd man out because i liked this

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: I know this comment is coming really late, but shouldn't the date formatting in this article be changed for continuity purposes? I'm pretty sure the most common date formatting on this site is dd/mm/yyyy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The concept here is just too daft to take seriously. I mean, with inscriptions as vague/open to interpretation as "the worst thing that will ever happen to anybody", it would be fairly difficult, if not impossible, to determine the accuracy of the SCP's predictions. Moreover, a lot of these inscriptions describe things that do not necessarily happen instantaneously, usually because there isn't a clear idea of what those particular processes actually entail. At what point does an organism actually cease to exist? When exactly does death occur - is it a process, or a single moment in time? Thus, I find the core concept to problematic, and the addenda don't help to clear up my concerns.

Also, the redaction (among the most heavy-handed of obfuscation methods) just takes away too much from this for me feel anything about it. So it's predicting the end of "The SCP Foundation" at some point in the future; alright, it's a little saddening, but everything has to end eventually. I hardly consider the notion that it will end (which is all this is telling us) a shocking or insightful thought (plus, a name-change would probably be all the Foundation would need when the time comes).

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at anytime.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

So there are quite a few areas for potential crosslinking to be found in this article. SCP-094 has been crosslinked to this article as part of the GCL, and could stand to be crosslinked back to. SCP-2305 crosslinks to this article in its list of potential neutralization attempts that go array, and could be linked to as part of a "don't tamper warning" in the containment procedures. The timer on SCP-2798 could be referenced on one of the hands in the log. As such, I am proposing the following edits to include these three ideas:

Changing this:

SCP-1032 is to be stored in a high-value inanimate-item cell at Sector-19. One unarmed guard is to be posted inside the cell at all times.

To this:

SCP-1032 is to be stored in a high-value inanimate-item cell at Sector-19. One unarmed guard is to be posted inside the cell at all times to prevent tampering.

And adding the following two entries to the log:

SCP-1032-15
Current text: "Effective Containment"
Predicted date: 09/04/2690
Notes: SCP-1032-15 will achieve its "midnight event" at approximately the same date as SCP-094 will fully engulf the Earth at its current rate of expansion.

SCP-1032-16
Current text: "Project Serapis"
Predicted date: 02/11/2016
Notes: [DATA EXPUNGED]

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why are there 22 hands? Why not 24?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Given that it's WELL past 09/01/2015, I made a change to the article updating the description of SCP-1032-3, as well as it's object class. Given that I was the person who made the original change to SCP-1032-3's description, I kinda assumed I had permission to make this change.

Personally, I think making it say "My Keter Classification" is funnier than just changing it back to safe, while still keeping in the spirit of the original article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-1032-02 sold me. The possibilities/subtext there are too horrifying to ignore. +1

item.title: Staff Post - Reversion Notice
item.content:encoded: Revision 32 was reverted back to Revision 31 due to making substantial changes without the author's apparent approval. In the future, please ask permission from the author for making substantial changes (IE, beyond grammar/spelling).

item.title: THE END IS NIGH
item.content:encoded: THE END IS COMING, AS PREDICTED BY A CLOCK

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This isn't tagged collaboration, so you can't add to it directly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, but why a tale instead of editing the actual article itself?

Yes, I know that some expungements are meant to remain unknown. All I want to know is if that is indeed the case here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good idea. Heck, why not just blackbox the 2015 date so that it's kept in a "floating timeline"-like state in the first place?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You can write pretty much anything as a tale, including supplements for other articles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If the re-edit thing is going through, why not just blackbox the date itself? Permanently saves the hassle of having to readjust the article every time the real life date passes the one in the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know, I've been wondering about the expungement of the logs for hands #14 to #19. Is it for brevity's sake, or because you couldn't come up with something at the time? If the latter, are others allowed to take up of writing entries for those hands?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes. I request permission to update the Object Class and SCP-1032-03.

If the request passes, then I would suggest putting the new date quite far off in time — like, a a decade or two from now. You know, to cut down on the number of times we have to change this, as well as avoid having that entry grow too long in comparison to the rest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes. I request permission to update the Object Class and SCP-1032-03.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was going to ask the same thing

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, shouldn't the classification be changing now? Or should this article be another "trapped in time" one?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really good and aged as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is my first post since joining the forums, so please excuse me if I'm breaching etiquette by commenting on an old post.

I think the clock referencing Foundation protocol is acceptable here. I would go so far as to say it is the primary reason why I am upvoting this article. The wording is clever. I appreciate how the doctor points out that it could become keter or safe based on the reclassification request, and proceeds to request it be reclassified as safe to potentially avoid causing more problems. Choosing to submit to an inevitable cycle of reclassification rather than invite another keter SCP in defiance. It seems like a nice little slice of Foundation life. I can imagine the researchers changing its classification back and forth every time the hand goes around. Like clockwork. Very appropriate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Unbeknownst to all but the most high-ranking staff, SCP-1032-15 actually reads "SCP report leaked on link sharing website".

It passed the 12 o'clock position earlier today.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: #12 won it for me.
Hopefully the last doomsday predicting SCP I will upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know, I am glad that even after a year and a half of lurking there are still hidden gems to be found that still surprise me.

Like this one. This one is frightening and holds quite the subtext, like Drewbear and others had pointed out.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: But that means there's a date to be expunged.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Y'know, I didn't realize it until I read it over a few times, but…

I really really friggin' love this article. And I have no idea why.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: #12s log made my upvote lol

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Here's what made this for me: the ambiguity of predictions being a commencement or cessation. If you could find a way to tweak the Foundation prediction so it could be interpreted as either a real boon (no more anomalous objects ever found?) or tragedy, with a hint of the actions preparing for the event causing it, that would be magnificent. Could be quite tricky, though.

Also: for some reason, the third paragraph of the description made me think of the line "We're going to have to do a number of things, almost immediately," from The Thick of It, and anything that invokes that programme is a wonderful thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nope, the Universe one is just expunged. We don't know how fast it's moving.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this one. It could just have been another "thing what makes disasters", but is well written enough to be interesting.

One thing that seems interesting: If I'm reading this right, the end of the universe hand is moving faster than than the "worst thing that will happen to anybody" hand, since its date can actually be predicted. Wouldn't this imply that the worst thing that will happen to anybody will come after the end of the universe (or whatever it means by "universe")?
